objections.description: Hardcoded 2-hop traversal is arbitrary and insufficient for investigation-grade legal research. Real workflow value exists for simple adjacency lists, but the implementation lacks: (1) configurable depth limits (N-hop transitive closure) for tracing regulatory cascades, (2) cycle detection to handle circular citations common in legal corpora, (3) edge weighting or pruning to prevent noise explosion in highly connected graphs. Current 'second hop' is a superficial list expansion, not graph exploration.
objections.suggested_fix: Implement recursive traversal with configurable maxDepth (default 3-5), hash-based visited-node tracking to prevent cycles, and TF-IDF or citation-count edge scoring to rank related documents by relevance before returning results.

objections.description: Mobile implementation prioritizes trigger placement over workflow ergonomics. 'Floating trigger' and 'bottom-nav' solve discoverability but fail field-use requirements for DOU research: no gesture-based dismissal (swipe down), no VirtualKeyboard API integration to prevent input occlusion on small viewports, no haptic feedback for selection confirmation, and likely missing ARIA live regions for screen reader announcements of dynamic results. Shallow mobile adaptation.
objections.suggested_fix: Implement bottom-sheet pattern with react-spring for drag-to-dismiss physics, use visualViewport API to anchor input field above on-screen keyboard, add vibration API calls on selection, and ensure WCAG 2.1 compliance with proper focus management and live regions.

objections.description: Refactoring into a library module improves code organization but the modeling remains shallow for legal investigation. 'Timeline building' likely produces linear event logs without temporal validity intervals (validFrom/validUntil). Missing critical features: (1) handling of overlapping/concurrent statuses (e.g., partial revocation), (2) conflict resolution rules (lex posterior, lex specialis), (3) temporal queries ('status as of date X'). Without these, researchers cannot perform historical validity analysis.
objections.suggested_fix: Model LegalStatus as intervals with explicit validFrom/validUntil timestamps, implement Allen algebra relations for temporal logic, add conflict resolution engine with configurable priority rules, and expose query interface getStatusAtDate(documentId, date) for point-in-time reconstruction.

objections.description: Rendering 'model evidence' without anchor links to specific text spans prevents verification. Investigators require provenance tracking to audit why a status was assigned. Current implementation likely displays derived conclusions without traceability to primary source paragraphs, making it unsuitable for litigation or compliance verification workflows.
objections.suggested_fix: Add span-level annotations with byte-offset or paragraph coordinates linking evidence to document text, implement highlight-on-hover synchronization between evidence panel and document view, and include confidence score provenance (which algorithms/metrics contributed to the score).

suggestions.description: Add interactive force-directed or hierarchical graph visualization (D3.js or Canvas) instead of list views for multi-hop results. Lists hide topological relationships; graph views reveal citation clusters and authority hubs essential for legal research.

Implement Service Worker caching for command palette history and recent graph traversals. DOU researchers often work in low-connectivity environments; offline access to recent investigations is critical workflow value.

Build export functionality for legal status timelines (PDF/JSON) with full audit trails including evidence hashes and version timestamps. Next priority should be differential analysis: compare status evolution between two dates to surface exactly what changed.
